summary:
Poliosis circumscripta refers to a localized, depigmented area in hair or skin resulting from the loss of melanin, which manifests as a white or unpigmented patch. While "circumscripta" indicates confinement to a single site, the condition still falls under the category of hypopigmentary changes involving visible white spots.  

(Note: The summary focuses only on explicit clinical definitions derived from available sources.)
